response.justification: The candidate has extensive experience in software development with diverse technologies, including C/C++ (used at National Instruments and for personal projects like sound positioning server), but lacks recent, focused C++ experience in industrial automation or cybersecurity contexts. While their background includes high-performance systems (e.g., low-latency apps with DLang, micro-services handling millions of requests), there is no clear evidence of work with message brokers, IoT protocols (MQTT/REST), or industrial cybersecurity standards (IEC 62443). Their strengths lie in polyglot development and agile practices, but relevance to the specific role is limited.
strengths: Strong C/C++ experience (including STL and object-oriented principles) demonstrated in prior roles and personal projects
Proven ability to design and optimize high-performance, scalable systems (e.g., real-time metadata handling, low-latency applications)
Experience with agile methodologies, secure coding practices (TDD, CI/CD), and mentorship (e.g., Refactoring Dojos, Selenium course)
concerns: No direct experience with industrial automation protocols (Modbus TCP/IP, IEC 61850, OPC UA) or cloud platform solutions (Azure)
Limited exposure to industrial cybersecurity standards (IEC 62443) or Secure Development Lifecycle (SDL) in relevant contexts
Recent roles focus on web/mobile development (Node.js, TypeScript, React) with minimal C++ work in the last 5+ years
